data.title: Buy Rating Reiterated on Nicox as NCX 470 Progress Supports Unchanged €1 Price Target
data.description:
In a report released today, Ram Selvaraju from H.C. Wainwright reiterated a Buy rating on Nicox SA, with a price target of €1.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Ram Selvaraju has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Nicox’s lead asset NCX 470 and its improving regulatory profile. He cites the recent U.S. NDA submission for NCX 470 in glaucoma, backed by two successful Phase 3 trials, as a major value inflection point that could lead to FDA approval by mid-2027 and commercial launch before year-end 2027.

He also emphasizes that development and commercialization expenses are borne by partners Kowa and Ocumension, while Nicox retains rights to milestone payments and tiered royalties, including a sizable potential milestone pool of €127M. Selvaraju’s valuation, which assumes an 85% probability of approval for NCX 470 and results in a firm value consistent with a €1 per share target price, supports maintaining the Buy rating despite typical regulatory, commercial, and dilution risks.

data.title: Bloom Energy: Brookfield AI Deal Expansion and Growing Project Pipeline Underpin Upgraded $310 Price Target and Buy Rating
data.description:
Morgan Stanley analyst David Arcaro has maintained their bullish stance on BE stock, giving a Buy rating today.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

Two ETFs for long or short leverage on BE

David Arcaro has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Bloom Energy’s expanding growth prospects and strengthened strategic partnerships. The fivefold increase of the Brookfield AI infrastructure agreement to $25 billion, implying up to 5 GW of potential fuel cell developments, signals robust, long-duration demand and validates Bloom’s positioning in powering data centers.

He also highlights Brookfield’s existing capital commitments and the rising pipeline of both mid-sized and gigawatt-scale projects as evidence that market opportunities are exceeding prior expectations. Combined with recent multi-gigawatt project wins with major customers and indications that additional announcements could justify capacity well beyond 5 GW by 2030, Arcaro sees a clear path for upside to current forecasts that underpins his positive investment stance on the stock.

In another report released today, Evercore ISI also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $350.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: UnitedHealthcare’s Early Guardant Shield Coverage Spurs Analyst to Raise Guardant Health Price Target to $190 and Reaffirm Buy Rating
data.description:
Guardant Health, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Mark Massaro from BTIG reiterated a Buy rating on the stock and has a $190.00 price target.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Mark Massaro has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, chiefly the unexpected early coverage of Guardant Shield by UnitedHealthcare, which significantly reduces reimbursement risk and accelerates the company’s screening opportunity. He views this decision, coming well before prior expectations and ahead of many peers, as a strong validation of Guardant’s technology and the growing influence of ACS screening guidelines, prompting him to raise his price target from $160 to $190.

He also anticipates that other major commercial insurers will likely mirror UnitedHealthcare’s move, expanding Shield’s addressable market and supporting higher long‑term revenue assumptions. Combined with existing upside from Guardant’s G360CDx franchise and a valuation based on robust 2028 revenue forecasts, Massaro believes the risk‑reward profile remains attractive, justifying his reiterated Buy rating and designation of Guardant Health as a top pick.

According to TipRanks, Massaro is a 2-star analyst with an average return of 0.7% and a 37.40% success rate. Massaro covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Guardant Health, Personalis, and SOPHiA GENETICS.

In another report released today, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $150.00 price target.

data.title: Bloom Energy Secures Buy Rating as Analyst Highlights Expanded $25B Brookfield Partnership and AI Data Center Power Opportunity; Price Target Raised to $350
data.description:
UBS analyst Manav Gupta has assigned their bullish stance on BE stock, giving a Buy rating today.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

High conviction on BE? Try Tradr's BEX or BEZ

Manav Gupta has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Bloom Energy’s strategic positioning and partnerships. He highlights the expanded fuel cell partnership with Brookfield, which grew from $5 billion to $25 billion as part of a $100 billion AI infrastructure initiative, positioning Bloom as a key onsite power provider for next-generation AI data centers.

This expansion underscores Bloom Energy’s role in supplying reliable, dispatchable power where total delivered cost and uptime matter more than just headline generation costs. Gupta argues that hyperscalers will increasingly favor integrated solutions that reduce system-level expenses, and he believes Bloom’s technology and partnerships place it in a strong position to capture premium economics within this evolving procurement framework.

In another report released today, Evercore ISI also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $350.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Brookfield Deal Expands Bloom Energy’s Financing Visibility but Early-Stage Execution Risks Keep Ameet Thakkar at Hold with Unchanged $279 Target
data.description:
In a report released today, Ameet Thakkar from BMO Capital maintained a Hold rating on Bloom Energy, with a price target of $279.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

Two ETFs to trade BE long or short: BEX & BEZ

Ameet Thakkar has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including the expanded $25 billion framework agreement with Brookfield, which materially improves financing visibility but does not yet translate into firm, contracted backlog. The larger program provides a supportive capital structure that can reduce funding hurdles for data center customers and broaden Bloom Energy’s opportunity set, yet the ultimate level and timing of deployments remain dependent on deal structure, tax credit treatment, and revenue mix outcomes.

At the same time, Thakkar notes that while order momentum and growth are running ahead of prior expectations, Bloom’s solid oxide fuel cell platform is still at an early commercialization stage, especially for behind-the-meter baseload applications where large-scale field performance is not fully proven. This combination of strengthening demand signals with meaningful execution and technology risk leads him to a balanced stance, concluding that the upside from the Brookfield expansion is offset by uncertainties around real-world scalability and long-term adoption, justifying a Hold recommendation with an unchanged price target of $279.

In another report released today, Roth MKM also maintained a Hold rating on the stock with a $285.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating Reiterated on Arrowhead as Cardiometabolic RNAi Portfolio Advances; $100 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
Analyst Patrick Trucchio from H.C. Wainwright reiterated a Buy rating on Arrowhead Pharmaceuticals and keeping the price target at $100.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock powerful investing tools and data-driven insights with TipRanks Premium for more confident investment decisions.
  • Discover top stock picks and new investment opportunities through TipRanks' Smart Investor Newsletter.

Patrick Trucchio has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Arrowhead’s advancing cardiometabolic portfolio and RNAi platform. He views the company’s siRNA-based programs—REDEMPLO, zodasiran, and ARO-DIMER-PA—as addressing a broad spectrum of triglyceride and LDL-C disorders with strong, durable lipid reductions and favorable tolerability, reinforcing Arrowhead’s position as a leading RNA interference innovator.

He also highlights several upcoming value drivers, including pivotal Phase 3 readouts for REDEMPLO in severe hypertriglyceridemia, Phase 3 enrollment progress for zodasiran, and initial human data for ARO-DIMER-PA, all expected over the next 12–18 months. In addition, the recent European Commission approval of REDEMPLO for familial chylomicronemia syndrome is seen as an important commercial milestone that broadens the company’s market reach, supporting his unchanged $100 price target and continued Buy recommendation.

In another report released yesterday, TD Cowen also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $0.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
